annotations.label: Covariant derivative (lead)

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative is a way of specifying a derivative along tangent vectors of a manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib's `CovariantDerivative` structure defines a bundled Koszul connection on an arbitrary vector bundle, generalizing the tangent-bundle case in the lead.

annotations.label: Covariant transformation law

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative transforms covariantly under a general coordinate transformation

annotations.note: Mathlib's covariant derivative is coordinate-free; there is no lemma stating the transformation law of Christoffel symbols under coordinate changes.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative as a rule on vectors and vector fields

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative is a rule

annotations.note: `IsCovariantDerivativeOn` axiomatizes the rule assigning to a section `σ` and vector `X x` the vector `∇_X σ x`, matching the informal rule-based definition.

annotations.label: Particle in polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: Consider the example of a particle moving along a curve

annotations.note: This motivating physics example (acceleration in polar coordinates) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parallel transport on a globe

anchor.snippet: A vector on a globe on the equator at point

annotations.note: Parallel transport itself is absent from Mathlib, so this expository example on the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: Infinitesimal parallel transport measures curvature

anchor.snippet: This infinitesimal change of the vector is a measure of the curvature

annotations.note: Neither parallel transport nor the Riemann curvature tensor is defined in Mathlib, so this qualitative curvature statement is unformalized.

annotations.label: Existence of Levi-Civita connection

anchor.snippet: for each metric there is a unique torsion -free covariant derivative called the Levi-Civita connection

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsMetricCompatible` and `CovariantDerivative.torsion`, but the existence/uniqueness theorem for the Levi-Civita connection itself is not yet proved.

annotations.label: Locality of covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: depends on the values of u in a neighborhood of a point

annotations.note: `congr_of_eventuallyEq` proves `cov σ x = cov σ' x` when `σ` and `σ'` agree on a neighborhood of `x`, i.e. `∇σ` depends only on the germ.

annotations.label: Embedding setup for covariant derivative

anchor.section: Informal definition using an embedding into Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: Suppose an open subset

annotations.note: Mathlib takes an intrinsic vector-bundle approach; the concrete embedded-submanifold-in-ℝⁿ setup used here is not developed.

annotations.label: Decomposition via Christoffel symbols

anchor.section: Informal definition using an embedding into Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: The last term is not tangential to M

annotations.note: Christoffel symbols are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Levi-Civita covariant derivative as orthogonal projection

anchor.section: Informal definition using an embedding into Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: is defined as the orthogonal projection of the usual derivative onto tangent space

annotations.note: The extrinsic tangential-projection construction of the Levi-Civita connection is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Christoffel symbols in terms of the metric

anchor.section: Informal definition using an embedding into Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: Thus the Christoffel symbols for the Levi-Civita connection are related to the metric

annotations.note: The Koszul-type formula expressing Christoffel symbols via metric derivatives is not formalized.

annotations.label: Circle rolled into a cylinder

anchor.section: Informal definition using an embedding into Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: For a very simple example that captures the essence of the description above

annotations.note: This illustrative example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative as a Koszul connection

anchor.snippet: A covariant derivative is a (Koszul) connection on the tangent bundle

annotations.note: The docstring of `CovariantDerivative` explicitly calls it a Koszul connection, defined on any vector bundle (thus generalizing the tangent-bundle case).

annotations.label: Compatibility with tensor product and trace

anchor.snippet: in a unique way that ensures compatibility with the tensor product and trace operations

annotations.note: Mathlib does not yet define the induced covariant derivative on tensor products / traces of bundles (mentioned as a TODO in `Metric.lean`).

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a function

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative of f at p along v is the scalar at p

annotations.note: Mathlib's `mfderiv` gives the differential of a function on a manifold, which coincides with the covariant derivative on functions, but is not packaged under that name.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of f along a vector field

anchor.snippet: is the function that associates with each point

annotations.note: This is `x ↦ mfderiv I 𝓘(𝕜) f x (X x)` in Mathlib, but there is no dedicated declaration for the pointwise-along-a-vector-field construction.

annotations.label: Equality with Lie and exterior derivative on functions

anchor.snippet: coincides with the Lie derivative

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a defined Lie derivative on scalar functions and any statement equating it with the covariant/exterior derivative on functions.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a vector field

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative of u at p along v is the tangent vector at p

annotations.note: Taking `V = TangentSpace I` in `CovariantDerivative I E V` gives the covariant derivative of a vector field along another; `cov σ x (X x)` corresponds to `∇_X σ x`.

annotations.label: Leibniz (product) rule for covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: obeys the product rule

annotations.note: The `leibniz` field of `IsCovariantDerivativeOn` is exactly the product rule `cov (g • σ) = g • cov σ + (d g).smulRight σ`.

annotations.label: Dependence on a neighborhood

anchor.snippet: depends not only on the value of u at p but also on values of u in a neighborhood

annotations.note: The proved germ-locality lemma `congr_of_eventuallyEq` captures dependence only on a neighborhood of the point.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a vector field on a domain

anchor.snippet: denotes the vector field whose value at each point

annotations.note: `IsCovariantDerivativeOn` takes a set parameter `s : Set M`, giving a covariant-derivative rule restricted to a domain.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a covector field

anchor.snippet: is defined as the unique one-form at p such that the following identity is satisfied

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the induced covariant derivative on the cotangent bundle.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of covector is covector

anchor.snippet: The covariant derivative of a covector field along a vector field v is again a covector field

annotations.note: No covector-field covariant derivative is defined, so its type-preserving property is not stated.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a tensor field

anchor.snippet: it can be defined for arbitrary tensor fields by imposing the following identities

annotations.note: The extension of the covariant derivative to general tensor fields via tensor-product and trace compatibility is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Explicit formula for covariant derivative of tensor

anchor.snippet: Explicitly, let T be a tensor field of type

annotations.note: No such coordinate formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Connection coefficients (Christoffel symbols)

anchor.snippet: are the components of the connection with respect to a system of local coordinates

annotations.note: Christoffel symbols / connection coefficients are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate formula for covariant derivative of vector field

anchor.snippet: Then using the rules in the definition

annotations.note: No index-formula `(∇_j v)^i = ∂_j v^i + Γ^i_{jk} v^k` is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate formula for covectors

anchor.snippet: For covectors similarly we have

annotations.note: No coordinate formula for covariant derivatives of covector fields is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coordinate formula for tensor fields

anchor.snippet: The covariant derivative of a type

annotations.note: No general (r,s)-tensor coordinate formula is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tensor density correction term

anchor.snippet: If instead of a tensor, one is trying to differentiate a tensor density

annotations.note: Tensor densities are not treated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Determinant of metric as scalar density

anchor.snippet: is a scalar density (of weight +1)

annotations.note: The weight-1 scalar density from `det g` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Semicolon/comma notation

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative is given with a semicolon

annotations.note: The classical `T^i_{;j}` / `T^i_{,j}` index notation is not adopted in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiple semicolon indices

anchor.snippet: In case two or more indexes appear after the semicolon

annotations.note: Iterated-semicolon notation is a syntactic convention absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Double pipe notation (Adler, Bazin & Schiffer)

anchor.snippet: the covariant derivative is denoted by a double pipe

annotations.note: Alternative double-pipe notation is not adopted.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a scalar field

anchor.snippet: covariant differentiation is simply partial differentiation

annotations.note: Mathlib has `mfderiv` for functions but no statement identifying the covariant derivative of a scalar with partial differentiation in coordinates.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a contravariant vector field

anchor.snippet: For a contravariant vector field

annotations.note: No coordinate-index expansion of `∇_μ T^ν` is available.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a covariant vector field

anchor.snippet: For a covariant vector field

annotations.note: No coordinate expression for `∇_μ T_ν` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a (2,0) tensor field

anchor.snippet: For a type (2,0) tensor field

annotations.note: Coordinate formula for `∇_μ T^{αβ}` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a (0,2) tensor field

anchor.snippet: For a type (0,2) tensor field

annotations.note: Coordinate formula for `∇_μ T_{αβ}` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative of a (1,1) tensor field

anchor.snippet: For a type (1,1) tensor field

annotations.note: Coordinate formula for `∇_μ T^α_β` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann tensor via commutator of covariant derivatives

anchor.snippet: The Riemann tensor

annotations.note: The Riemann curvature tensor is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutator on (2,0)-tensor field

anchor.snippet: The covariant derivative of a (2,0)-tensor field fulfills

annotations.note: Without a Riemann tensor, the Ricci identity for (2,0) tensor commutators is absent.

annotations.label: Covariant derivative along a smooth curve

anchor.snippet: one can define the covariant derivative along a smooth curve

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the covariant derivative operator `D/dt` acting on curves' vector fields.

annotations.label: Geodesic of the covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: then the curve is called a geodesic of the covariant derivative

annotations.note: Geodesics of a connection are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesics of Levi-Civita are metric geodesics

anchor.snippet: the geodesics for the connection are precisely the geodesics of the metric

annotations.note: Neither Levi-Civita geodesics nor Riemannian metric geodesics are available in Mathlib's manifold library.

annotations.label: Absolute/intrinsic derivative

anchor.snippet: Sometimes the covariant derivative along a curve is called absolute or intrinsic derivative

annotations.note: An alternate name for the (unformalized) covariant derivative along a curve.

annotations.label: Antisymmetrized covariant derivative vs Lie derivative

anchor.snippet: the antisymmetrized covariant derivative

annotations.note: `torsion_eq_zero_iff` states `∇_X Y − ∇_Y X = [X,Y]` iff torsion vanishes, which is the torsion-free case of the antisymmetrized-covariant-vs-Lie identity, but the general formula with a torsion term is not stated.
